## openclaw.json Structure

Path on host: `/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/openclaw.json`
Path in manager container: `/root/manager-workspace/openclaw.json`
Path in controller container: `/root/hiclaw-fs/agents/manager/openclaw.json`

This file is the live runtime config for the OpenClaw gateway running inside `hiclaw-manager`. It has three concurrent writers:

- `start-manager-agent.sh` — rewrites key fields at every container start
- `manager-config-keeper.sh` — enforces schema invariants every minute from host cron
- The gateway's ManagerReconciler — writes its template to the file every ~47 seconds

OpenClaw also maintains a backup at `workspace/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak` and a health baseline at `workspace/.openclaw/logs/config-health.json`. Both are used by the observe-recovery mechanism to revert "unexpected" changes — `manager-config-keeper.sh` updates both files atomically whenever it edits `openclaw.json`.

### `gateway`

```json
"gateway": {
  "auth": { "token": "[REDACTED]" },
  "bind": "lan",
  "mode": "local",
  "port": 18799,
  "remote": { "token": "[REDACTED]" },
  "controlUi": {
    "allowInsecureAuth": true,
    "allowedOrigins": ["*"],
    "dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth": true
  }
}
```

`auth.token` and `remote.token` are both set to `HICLAW_MANAGER_GATEWAY_KEY` by the startup script. They are also injected verbatim into the nginx `manager-console.conf` sub_filter for auto-login token injection (see [nginx Upstream Resolution](#nginx-upstream-resolution)).

**WARNING — `controlUi` flags:**

| Flag | Current value | Why it is set | Safe to remove? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `allowInsecureAuth` | `true` | Allows the control UI to authenticate over plain HTTP without TLS. Required because the gateway runs on `http://` internally; TLS is terminated at Traefik. | Do not remove — removing it causes login failures when accessed via `control.claw.designflow.app`. |
| `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth` | `true` | Disables per-device auth enforcement in the control UI. Required because the auto-login mechanism injects a single shared token rather than issuing per-device credentials. | Do not remove unless you implement per-device token issuance. Removing it causes the injected token to fail device auth and the control UI becomes inaccessible. |
| `allowedOrigins` | `["*"]` | Allows the Control UI to be embedded from any origin. Required for Traefik's cross-origin proxy. | Can be tightened to `["https://control.claw.designflow.app"]` if cross-origin abuse is a concern — but test thoroughly before restricting. |

### `channels.matrix`

```json
"channels": {
  "matrix": {
    "accessToken": "[REDACTED] value — set by startup script>",
    "autoJoin": "always",
    "blockStreaming": true,
    "dm": {
      "allowFrom": ["@manager:...", "@admin:..."],
      "policy": "allowlist"
    },
    "enabled": true,
    "encryption": false,
    "groupAllowFrom": ["@manager:...", "@admin:..."],
    "groupPolicy": "allowlist",
    "groups": {},
    "homeserver": "http://hiclaw-controller:6167",
    "network": { "dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork": true },
    "streaming": "partial",
    "userId": "@manager:matrix-local.hiclaw.io:18080"
  }
}
```

`accessToken` and `homeserver` are overwritten by the startup script on every start using the value obtained from `POST /login` against the Tuwunel homeserver. Do not hand-edit these — they are valid for the current Matrix session only.

`channels.matrix.groups` must never contain a wildcard key `"*"`. The OpenClaw schema rejects additional properties in the groups object, causing every config reload and `update.run` to fail with `managed-service-handoff-unavailable`. The config keeper strips this key on every run.

`network.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork` is required because the Matrix homeserver is on a private Docker network. Without it the gateway refuses to connect to non-routable IPs.

### `session`

```json
"session": {
  "dmScope": "main",
  "resetByType": {
    "dm": { "atHour": 4, "mode": "daily" },
    "group": { "atHour": 4, "mode": "daily" }
  }
}
```

`session.dmScope = "main"` forces all Matrix DMs onto the agent's primary session key (`agent:main:main`), so the HiClaw chat UI and OpenClaw web chat share one transcript. The config keeper enforces this value. Do not change it to `"per-dm"` — doing so creates isolated session contexts per DM thread and the two UIs diverge.

`resetByType` schedules daily session resets at 04:00 UTC for both DM and group sessions.

### `commands`

```json
"commands": { "restart": true }
```

**This is the most non-obvious part of the system. Read this before touching anything related to `commands`.**

The gateway uses a startup-time baseline for reload diffs, not its in-memory running state. When the container starts, `start-manager-agent.sh` writes `commands.restart = true` before launching the gateway. The gateway records this as its permanent "last known good" in `config-health.json`. Every subsequent file change is diffed against that startup baseline — not against the current in-memory state.

If `commands` changes in the diff, the gateway triggers a full in-process restart (SIGUSR1) instead of a hot reload. The ManagerReconciler writes `commands: null` every ~47 seconds. When the keeper writes `commands: {"restart": true}` to match the startup baseline, the diff for `commands` is zero and no restart occurs.

| Keeper writes | Diff vs startup baseline | Result |
|---|---|---|
| `{"restart": true}` | No change | Hot reload only |
| `{}` | `commands.restart` removed | Full restart triggered |
| `null` | `commands` changed | Full restart triggered |
| `{"restart": false}` | `commands.restart` changed | Full restart triggered |

**Do not write any value other than `{"restart": true}` to `commands`.** A broken state produces a 5-minute restart loop visible in `docker logs hiclaw-manager` as repeated `[reload] config change requires gateway restart (commands.restart)` entries.

To inspect the startup baseline:

```bash
sudo python3 -c "
import json
h = json.load(open('/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/.openclaw/logs/config-health.json'))
for path, info in h['entries'].items():
    lg = info['lastKnownGood']
    print(path, '  hash:', lg['hash'][:16], ' bytes:', lg['bytes'])
"
```

To verify the keeper is writing the correct value:

```bash
sudo python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/openclaw.json')); print('commands:', d.get('commands'))"
# Expected: {'restart': True}
```

# Deployment

## What Deployment Means Here

There is no single release pipeline. Deployment splits into three tracks:

| Track | What changes | How it deploys |
|---|---|---|
| Container image | `novnc-desktop/` source | git push → GitHub Actions → GHCR → manual `docker pull` |
| Host scripts and configs | keeper scripts, oauth2-proxy, traefik | git push → `git pull` on server → manual service restart |
| Runtime config | `workspace/openclaw.json` | direct file edit or keeper script convergence |

hiclaw-manager and hiclaw-controller are **not in Coolify** and are **not rebuilt from this repo**. They come from Alibaba's registry and are kept alive by host-side keeper scripts running as cron jobs.

---

## Domain Map

| URL | Backend | Auth |
|---|---|---|
| `claw.designflow.app` | hiclaw-controller port 8088 (Element Web + nginx) | Google OAuth (oauth2-proxy) |
| `control.claw.designflow.app` | hiclaw-controller port 18888 (nginx → hiclaw-manager:18799) | Google OAuth |
| `gateway.claw.designflow.app` | hiclaw-manager port 18799 (OpenClaw gateway UI) | Google OAuth |
| `vnc.designflow.app` | novnc-desktop port 3000 | None — no auth gate |

Traffic flows: browser → Traefik (`coolify-proxy`) → `oauth2-proxy` (Google OAuth gate) → backend container.

Routing config: `traefik/claw.yml` (copy of `/data/coolify/proxy/dynamic/claw.yml`). Traefik hot-reloads it — no Traefik restart needed after edits.

---

## Persistent Boundary

Everything on the host filesystem at `/worksp/hiclaw/` survives container recreation. Everything inside containers that is not backed by a bind mount or a keeper script does not.

| Survives container recreation | Does not survive |
|---|---|
| `workspace/` content (bind-mounted to `/root/manager-workspace` in manager) | `docker exec`-made changes with no keeper backing |
| `workspace/openclaw.json` | npm packages installed inside container overlay |
| Host keeper and startup scripts | `/usr/local/bin/mc` wrapper (installed by `fix-element-config.sh`; re-installed on next controller restart) |
| `oauth2-proxy/` config | `/usr/local/bin/npm` wrapper (reinstalled by `start-manager-agent.sh` on startup) |
| `traefik/claw.yml` | nginx configs generated inside controller (regenerated by `start-element-web.sh` on restart) |
| `novnc-desktop/` volume (`novnc-e10kwzww46ljhrgz1qj08j6a-config`) | clawtalk in-container modifications (see AGENTS.md § Pending Work) |

## GitHub Actions Pipeline (novnc-desktop only)

**Trigger:** push to `main` when any file under `novnc-desktop/` changes, or `workflow_dispatch`.

**Stages:**
1. `quality` — `shellcheck novnc-desktop/novnc-startup.sh` + `ruff check novnc-desktop/cdp_proxy.py`
2. `build` — `docker buildx build`, pushed to GHCR as:
   - `ghcr.io/u2giants/novnc-desktop:latest`
   - `ghcr.io/u2giants/novnc-desktop:sha-<commit-sha>`
3. `post-build` — calls Coolify API to restart the novnc service. **This step always fails** (the Coolify service was deleted 2026-05-10). Ignore the error — the image is built and pushed correctly regardless.

The CI build does not deploy hiclaw-manager or hiclaw-controller. Those images come from Alibaba's registry.

## Required Host Cron Jobs

All four must be present in the `ai` user's crontab. Verify with `sudo crontab -u ai -l`.

```cron
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/manager-config-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/manager-config-keeper.log 2>&1
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/manager-bootstrap-keeper.log 2>&1
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/controller-bootstrap-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/controller-bootstrap-keeper.log 2>&1
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/novnc-resource-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/novnc-resource-keeper.log 2>&1
```

If any are missing, add them with `sudo crontab -u ai -e`. Missing keepers mean:
- manager startup script edits never reach the running container
- memory limits are never re-applied after container recreation
- novnc-desktop can run uncapped and cause host-wide Chrome/QtWebEngine OOM
- openclaw update marker is never consumed (UI "Update now" does nothing)
- openclaw.json drifts (wildcard groups key, stale contextWindow, observe-recovery reverts)

---

## Container Resource Limits

| Container | RAM | Total swap (--memory-swap) | CPUs | PID limit |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `hiclaw-manager` | 1536 MiB | 3072 MiB (~1.5 GiB of actual swap) | 1 | Docker default |
| `hiclaw-controller` | 3072 MiB | 4096 MiB (~1 GiB of actual swap) | 2 | 1024 |
| `novnc-desktop` | 3072 MiB | 4096 MiB (~1 GiB of actual swap) | 2 | 250 |

**Why manager swap is set high:** Docker's `--memory-swap` is the *total* of RAM + swap — not additive. With `--memory 1536m --memory-swap 1536m`, actual swap is 0 bytes. npm install during an openclaw update peaks well above 1536 MiB total and OOM-kills mid-install, leaving a broken partial install. Setting `--memory-swap 3g` gives ~1.5 GiB of actual swap headroom.

## Host Memory Policy

The host swapfile is `/swapfile`, mounted from `/etc/fstab`, sized at 12 GiB. Swappiness is pinned to `20` in `/etc/sysctl.d/99-hiclaw-memory.conf`.

This is a host-level safety valve only. The first line of defense is still container limits, especially `novnc-desktop` (`3g` RAM, `4g` total RAM+swap, 250 PIDs). Do not remove those limits just because the host has more swap; without them Chrome/QtWebEngine can still drive a global OOM and make SSH unreachable.

The keeper re-applies these limits after every container recreation:

```bash
# manager
docker update --memory 1536m --memory-swap 3g --cpus 1 hiclaw-manager

# controller
docker update --memory 3g --memory-swap 4g --cpus 2 --pids-limit 1024 hiclaw-controller

# novnc desktop
docker update --memory 3g --memory-swap 4g --cpus 2 --pids-limit 250 novnc-desktop
```

## Starting the System

### First-time or after both containers are stopped

```bash
cd /worksp/hiclaw

# Start controller first — MinIO, Matrix, and Element Web must be up before manager needs them
docker start hiclaw-controller
sleep 20

# Check for MinIO recursion BEFORE starting the manager (see architecture.md § MinIO Sync Safety)
sudo find /var/lib/docker/volumes/hiclaw-data/_data/minio/hiclaw-storage \
  -maxdepth 8 -type d -name "hiclaw-storage" -print
# Must print only the root path. Extra lines = recursion bug present.
# If extra lines appear: do NOT start manager — follow architecture.md § MinIO recovery.

docker start hiclaw-manager
sleep 30

# Verify MinIO still clean after manager startup
ls /worksp/hiclaw/workspace/hiclaw/ 2>/dev/null && echo "WARNING: recursion seed appeared" || echo "OK"

# Verify manager
docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw clawtalk doctor
bash /worksp/hiclaw/manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh
```

### novnc-desktop (or full recreate)

```bash
# Shortcut: bash /worksp/hiclaw/novnc-desktop/recreate.sh

docker pull ghcr.io/u2giants/novnc-desktop:latest
docker stop novnc-desktop && docker rm novnc-desktop
docker run -d --name novnc-desktop \
  --network e10kwzww46ljhrgz1qj08j6a --ip 10.0.5.4 \
  --dns 1.1.1.1 --dns 8.8.8.8 \
  --memory 3g --memory-swap 4g --cpus 2 --pids-limit 250 \
  -v novnc-e10kwzww46ljhrgz1qj08j6a-config:/config \
  -e PUID=1000 -e PGID=1000 -e TZ=UTC -e "TITLE=HiClaw Desktop" \
  --shm-size=2g --restart unless-stopped \
  ghcr.io/u2giants/novnc-desktop:latest
docker network connect coolify novnc-desktop
```

`--dns 1.1.1.1 --dns 8.8.8.8` is required: the host resolver uses Tailscale (`100.100.100.100`) which is unreachable from inside Docker containers — all DNS lookups fail without explicit public resolvers.

`--ip 10.0.5.4` is required: this address is hardcoded as the CDP endpoint for Playwright MCP.

**Rollback:** replace `:latest` with `:sha-<previous-commit>` in the `docker run` command.

## Normal Container Recreation (automatic)

When HiClaw recreates `hiclaw-manager` or `hiclaw-controller`, the keeper cron jobs converge within 60 seconds:

1. HiClaw creates a new container with the stock startup script.
2. The keeper detects the startup script hash mismatch (or new container ID).
3. The keeper `docker cp`s the host-owned patched script into the container and re-applies resource limits. No immediate restart — the patch takes effect on the container's next natural restart.
4. If the openclaw package hash also changed since the last boot, the keeper triggers `docker restart hiclaw-manager`.
5. On restart, `start-manager-agent.sh` runs: MinIO sync, ClawTalk bootstrap, whatsapp bootstrap, openclaw hash write, `exec openclaw gateway run`.

The keeper logs `container startup script not readable yet; skipping this run` while the container is still booting. This is expected.

### What the keeper does and does not restart for

| Change | Restart |
|---|---|
| `start-manager-agent.sh` content changed | No — patched silently; takes effect on next natural restart |
| openclaw `package.json` hash changed (after update) | Yes — `docker restart hiclaw-manager` |
| Container recreated with same scripts | Resource limits re-applied; no extra restart |
| `start-element-web.sh` or `start-tuwunel.sh` hash changed | Yes — `docker restart hiclaw-controller` |

A `git push` of a script change does not interrupt the active user session.

## openclaw Updates

### Normal flow (via Control UI)

1. Open `https://gateway.claw.designflow.app`
2. Click "Update now"
3. openclaw calls the fake `/usr/local/bin/systemd-run` (installed by `start-manager-agent.sh`), which writes `/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/.openclaw-update-requested` and exits 0
4. openclaw does an in-process SIGUSR1 restart — the container stays running
5. Within 60 seconds, `manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh` detects the marker, removes it, and runs inside the container:
   ```bash
   docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw update --yes --json
   ```
   with the container already set to `--memory 1536m --memory-swap 3g`
6. The keeper **waits 30 seconds** after the update command completes, allowing the in-process SIGUSR1 restart (including openclaw.json write) to finish before proceeding
7. The keeper then compares the current in-container `package.json` hash against the hash written at startup; if changed, it runs `docker restart hiclaw-manager`
8. On restart, `start-manager-agent.sh` validates the npm install (checks `json5/package.json` and `openai/index.mjs`), updates `/usr/local/bin/openclaw` to point to the npm binary, records the new hash

Verify after restart:

```bash
docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw --version
```

### Why the 30-second wait matters

openclaw uses an in-process SIGUSR1 restart (not exec-based, due to `OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1`). This restart includes a write phase where openclaw rewrites `openclaw.json` with the updated config. If `docker restart` is issued during this write window, the container is killed mid-write and leaves a truncated config file on the bind-mounted volume — a broken state that crashes the gateway on every subsequent startup.

The 30-second sleep in the keeper absorbs this window. **Do not shorten or remove it.**

### openclaw install validation

The startup script checks two files before symlinking the npm-installed openclaw:
- `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/json5/package.json`
- `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/openai/index.mjs`

If either is absent (OOM-interrupted partial install), the broken directory is deleted and the symlink falls back to the base image at `/opt/openclaw/`. This prevents the gateway from crashing at startup with `Cannot find module openai/index.mjs`.

### openclaw hash detection

`start-manager-agent.sh` writes a sha256 of the active openclaw `package.json` to:

```
/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash   (host path)
/root/manager-workspace/.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash    (container path — same file via bind mount)
```

`manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh` reads the host copy and compares it against the current in-container package hash. Both sides probe `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/package.json` first, then fall back to `/opt/openclaw/package.json`.

### Manual update (emergency only)

Only do this if the normal marker flow is not available.

```bash
# 1. Pause keeper crons
crontab -e    # comment out all three keeper lines

# 2. Ensure swap headroom
docker update --memory 1536m --memory-swap 3g --cpus 1 hiclaw-manager

# 3. Run update
docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw update --yes --json

# 4. Wait for in-process SIGUSR1 to finish

docker stop novnc-desktop && docker rm novnc-desktop
bash /worksp/hiclaw/novnc-desktop/recreate.sh

**Rollback:** use `:sha-<previous-commit>` instead of `:latest`.

# Then apply per-component:

## Environment Variables

**Manager env vars** are passed at `docker run` time from `start-manager-agent.sh`. The critical secrets (`HICLAW_MANAGER_GATEWAY_KEY`, `HICLAW_MANAGER_PASSWORD`) are auto-generated on first run and persisted to `/data/hiclaw-secrets.env` (chmod 600, bind-mounted from the host).

**Controller env vars** are set in the `docker run` command inside `start-manager-agent.sh` for the controller, and in the Coolify service definition for services that are Coolify-managed.

**novnc-desktop env vars** are set by the `docker run` command (see "Starting the System" above) and stored in the named volume.

**All variable names** are documented in `.env.example` at the repo root. Real values are never committed.

| Secret category | Storage location |
|---|---|
| Admin password (Higress + MinIO) | `/data/hiclaw-secrets.env` on host |
| OpenRouter API key | `/data/hiclaw-secrets.env` |
| Manager auth (gateway key, password, JWT) | `/data/hiclaw-secrets.env` |
| Matrix registration token | `/data/hiclaw-secrets.env` |
| MinIO secret key | `/data/hiclaw-secrets.env` |
| Google OAuth credentials | `oauth2-proxy/.env` (never committed) |
| openclaw API key + Matrix token | `workspace/openclaw.json` (runtime, not committed) |

## oauth2-proxy — Authentication Provider

Provider: Google OAuth direct (`--provider=google`). Authentik is not in the auth path.

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Provider | `google` |
| Redirect URI | `https://control.claw.designflow.app/oauth2/callback` |
| Cookie domain | `claw.designflow.app` and `*.claw.designflow.app` |
| Allowed emails | `oauth2-proxy/allowed-emails.txt` |
| Credentials | `oauth2-proxy/.env` (see `oauth2-proxy/.env.example`) |

To add an allowed email:

```bash
# Edit locally, commit, pull on server
echo "newuser@example.com" >> /worksp/hiclaw/oauth2-proxy/allowed-emails.txt
# commit and push, then on server:
cd /worksp/hiclaw && git pull
cd /worksp/hiclaw/oauth2-proxy && docker compose up -d
```

To rotate Google OAuth credentials: update `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` in `oauth2-proxy/.env`. The redirect URI `https://control.claw.designflow.app/oauth2/callback` must be registered in Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials → OAuth 2.0 Client IDs.

**After any oauth2-proxy change**, open an incognito window and verify Google SSO still completes and lands in the chat without a second login step.

## nginx Upstream for control.claw

nginx inside `hiclaw-controller` proxies `control.claw.designflow.app` → `hiclaw-manager:18799`. It must not use a hardcoded IP — Docker reassigns IPs on container recreation.

`start-element-web.sh` generates `manager-console.conf` with Docker's embedded DNS resolver:

```nginx
resolver 127.0.0.11 valid=10s;
set $upstream hiclaw-manager;
proxy_pass http://$upstream:18799;
```

By storing the hostname in a variable, nginx bypasses its startup-time DNS cache and re-resolves on each request.

If `control.claw.designflow.app` returns 502 after a manager restart:

```bash
# Verify the config is present and uses the variable form
docker exec hiclaw-controller cat /etc/nginx/conf.d/manager-console.conf

# If missing or hardcoded: restart the controller to regenerate it
docker restart hiclaw-controller
```

---

## Model Context Window Sync

On each manager startup, `start-manager-agent.sh` fetches the live model list from OpenRouter and updates `contextWindow` and `maxTokens` for matching model IDs in `openclaw.json`. The sync writes back to MinIO immediately so a subsequent MinIO→local pull does not overwrite the fresh values.

Models accessed via Higress gateway alias IDs (e.g. `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5-mini`) do not match OpenRouter IDs and keep their statically configured values. The deepseek models enforce a minimum context window via `manager-config-keeper.sh` even if the reconciler reverts them.

## Manual Recovery

If automation has not converged after two minutes:

```bash
bash /worksp/hiclaw/manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh
bash /worksp/hiclaw/manager-config-keeper.sh
bash /worksp/hiclaw/mcp-keeper.sh
docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw clawtalk doctor
```

If `openclaw.json` is corrupt or truncated (symptoms: gateway crashes at startup, keeper logs parse errors):

```bash
# Option 1: restore from last-good backup
cp /worksp/hiclaw/workspace/openclaw.json.bak /worksp/hiclaw/workspace/openclaw.json
docker restart hiclaw-manager
```

If the `.bak` file is also bad — check file size first (healthy `openclaw.json` is ~8.9 KB or larger; a truncated file is a few hundred bytes):

```bash
ls -lh /worksp/hiclaw/workspace/openclaw.json /worksp/hiclaw/workspace/openclaw.json.bak

# Option 2: restore from MinIO (reflects last successful startup sync)
docker exec hiclaw-manager mc cp hiclaw/hiclaw-storage/manager/openclaw.json \
  /root/manager-workspace/openclaw.json
docker restart hiclaw-manager
```

## Do Not Do

These actions will break the system:

| Action | Why it breaks things |
|---|---|
| `docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw update --yes` without pausing keepers | Config keeper fires mid-install and modifies openclaw.json; npm OOM risk without swap check |
| `docker restart hiclaw-manager` immediately after an openclaw update (within 30s) | Kills the container mid-write during the in-process SIGUSR1 restart; produces a truncated openclaw.json that crashes the gateway on every subsequent startup |
| `docker update --memory-swap 1536m hiclaw-manager` (same value as `--memory`) | Sets actual swap to 0 bytes; npm install OOM-kills the container during openclaw updates |
| Write or `cp` to `novnc-desktop/cdp_proxy.py` on the host | Creates a new inode; Docker bind mount still points to old inode; container never sees the update. Use Edit tool or `sed -i` instead. |
| Set `channels.matrix.groups["*"]` in openclaw.json | Rejected by OpenClaw schema; breaks `update.run` and every config reload |
| Leave `commands.restart=true` in openclaw.json without keeper running | Gateway triggers restart on next reconciler diff; causes a 5-minute restart loop |
| Deploy hiclaw-manager or hiclaw-controller via Coolify | They are not in Coolify. Coolify will not find them and will create duplicates. |
| Run `openclaw update` directly inside the container while the marker mechanism is working | Bypasses swap-headroom enforcement and the 30s grace window; risks truncation and conflicts with keeper |
| Set `--memory 1536m` without also increasing `--memory-swap` above 1536m | Equivalent to setting swap to 0 bytes; same OOM failure as above |

## 6. Storage

### Shared workspace volume

```
Host path:  /worksp/hiclaw/workspace/
Manager:    /root/manager-workspace/          (read/write — OpenClaw config, state, skills)
Controller: /root/hiclaw-fs/agents/manager/  (read/write — reconciler pushes this to MinIO)
```

Both containers see the same files simultaneously. Key files:

| File | Written by | Read by |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `openclaw.json` | manager startup, reconciler, keeper, gateway | gateway (live watch), keeper |
| `openclaw.json.bak` | observe-recovery | observe-recovery (restore trigger) |
| `openclaw.json.bak.[1-4]` | keeper (rotating backup) | keeper |
| `.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash` | manager startup | manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh |
| `.openclaw-update-requested` | fake systemd-run (in container) | manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh (host) |
| `.openclaw/logs/config-health.json` | gateway, keeper | gateway (observe-recovery), keeper |
| `state.json` | manager startup (DM room ID) | manager |
| `memory/` | agent memory writes | agent reads |
| `skills/` | upgrade-builtins.sh | agent |

### MinIO object storage

- **Endpoint:** `http://hiclaw-controller:9000` (from manager), `http://127.0.0.1:9000` (from controller)
- **Bucket:** `hiclaw-storage`
- **Key prefix:** `hiclaw/hiclaw-storage/`
- **Relevant paths:**
  - `manager/openclaw.json` — synced copy of workspace openclaw.json
  - `manager/skills/` — agent skill definitions
  - `manager/memory/` — agent memory files
  - `manager/openclaw.json.clobbered.*` — forensic copies from truncation events
  - `credentials/matrix/password` — worker Matrix passwords (uploaded by startup script)
- **Data directory on host:** `/var/lib/docker/volumes/hiclaw-data/_data/minio/`

### Persistent host paths

| Path | Contents | Survives |
|------|----------|---------|
| `/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/` | Runtime state, openclaw config | Container recreation (bind mount) |
| `/var/lib/docker/volumes/hiclaw-data/` | MinIO data, Tuwunel RocksDB | Container recreation (named volume) |
| `/data/hiclaw-secrets.env` | Auto-generated manager credentials | Reboots (host file) |
| `/data/worker-creds/<name>.env` | Worker Matrix passwords | Reboots (host file) |
| `/worksp/hiclaw/.state/` | Keeper last-container-ID tracking | Reboots (git-ignored) |

## 7. Update Mechanism

### Normal flow (via Control UI "Update now")

```
1. Admin clicks "Update now" in control.claw.designflow.app
2. OpenClaw calls update.run in-process
3. Because OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-gateway is set, openclaw follows
   managed-service-handoff path → calls /usr/local/bin/systemd-run
4. Fake systemd-run writes /root/manager-workspace/.openclaw-update-requested
   (host-visible as /worksp/hiclaw/workspace/.openclaw-update-requested)
   and exits 0
5. Gateway thinks handoff started; sends itself SIGUSR1 → in-process restart begins
6. manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh (host cron, ~60s interval) detects marker file:
   a. Removes marker
   b. Runs: docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw update --yes --json
      (--memory-swap 2g is set via docker update before this step)
   c. Sleeps 30s (allows in-process SIGUSR1 write to complete — prevents truncation)
7. Keeper computes hash of new /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/package.json
8. Hash differs from .openclaw-startup-pkg-hash → docker restart hiclaw-manager
9. start-manager-agent.sh validates new install (json5 + openai/index.mjs checks)
10. If valid: symlinks /usr/local/bin/openclaw → /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/
    If invalid: removes broken install, symlinks to /opt/openclaw/ (base image)
11. New hash written to .openclaw-startup-pkg-hash
```

**Why the 30s sleep is critical:** The `docker restart` immediately after `openclaw update` killed the container while openclaw was mid-write to `openclaw.json` during its SIGUSR1 in-process restart. This produced consistently truncated config files (8788 bytes vs 9283 bytes complete). The truncation corrupted config on every update cycle. The 30s sleep gives the write time to complete before the container is killed.

### SIGUSR1 in-process restart vs docker restart

| Event | Mechanism | openclaw.json written? | Container killed? |
|-------|-----------|----------------------|------------------|
| Config file change detected by file watcher | SIGUSR1 in-process | Yes (rewrites with current config) | No |
| `openclaw update --yes` | SIGUSR1 in-process | Yes (rewrites with new config) | No |
| Hash mismatch detected by keeper | `docker restart` | No (container is killed) | Yes |
| Manual `docker restart hiclaw-manager` | — | No | Yes |

`OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1` is set in the environment, which changes openclaw's restart behavior: on SIGUSR1, it restarts in-process (re-requiring all modules) rather than calling `exec` to replace the process. This is required for the fake systemd-run handoff to work correctly, but it means config writes happen mid-restart and are vulnerable to interruption by `docker restart`.

### Keeper orchestration

Host-side keeper scripts maintain container/runtime state:

| Script | Interval | Responsibility |
|--------|----------|---------------|
| `manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh` | ~60s | Sync start-manager-agent.sh into container; apply resource limits; consume update marker; detect version hash change |
| `manager-config-keeper.sh` | ~60s | Normalize openclaw.json; enforce invariants; defeat observe-recovery |
| `controller-bootstrap-keeper.sh` | ~60s | Sync start-element-web.sh and start-tuwunel.sh into controller; apply resource limits; restart if scripts changed |
| `novnc-resource-keeper.sh` | ~60s | Enforce noVNC Docker limits; restart before Chrome memory or PID growth can cause global OOM |
| `mcp-keeper.sh` | ~few min | Ensure mcp.servers.browser (Playwright CDP) is in openclaw.json |

## 8. OpenClaw Plugin Architecture

OpenClaw loads plugins from two locations:

1. **Bundled extensions:** `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/<plugin>/`
2. **Load paths:** `plugins.load.paths` in `openclaw.json` — additional directories scanned for plugin manifests

Plugins are declared in `plugins.entries` (with `enabled`, `apiKey`, `autoConnect` fields) and must appear in `plugins.allow` to be activated.

### Matrix channel plugin

Built into OpenClaw. Configured via `channels.matrix` in `openclaw.json`:
- Connects to Tuwunel at `http://hiclaw-controller:6167`
- Authenticates as `@manager:matrix-local.hiclaw.io:18080`
- Handles DMs from `allowFrom` list (`@admin`, `@manager`)
- `session.dmScope=main` collapses all DMs into `agent:main:main` session

### clawtalk plugin

- **npm package:** `/root/manager-workspace/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/clawtalk/`
- **Problem:** `build/index.js` is an ES module; OpenClaw uses CJS `require()`
- **Fix:** `start-manager-agent.sh` creates a CJS wrapper `index.cjs` that does `const m = require('./build/index.js'); module.exports = m.default || m;` and updates `package.json` to point at `index.cjs`
- **Bundled shim:** also created at `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/clawtalk/` on every startup
- **installs.json:** deleted on every startup so OpenClaw rescans and finds the shim; without deletion the cached registry predates the shim and reports "plugin not found"
- **This fix is ephemeral** — lives inside the container overlay and is lost on restart; `start-manager-agent.sh` recreates it on every start

### whatsapp plugin

- **npm package:** `@openclaw/whatsapp` — installed at openclaw version if absent
- Configured in `plugins.allow`, `plugins.load.paths`, and `channels.whatsapp`
- `dmPolicy=pairing`, `groupPolicy=allowlist`

### memory-core

Built into OpenClaw. Activated when `memorySearch` is configured in `openclaw.json` with an embedding model (set from `HICLAW_EMBEDDING_MODEL`).

### MCP browser plugin (Playwright)

Not a traditional OpenClaw plugin — configured as `mcp.servers.browser` in `openclaw.json`:
```json
{
  "mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "browser": {
        "command": "npx",
        "args": ["@playwright/mcp", "--cdp-endpoint", "http://10.0.5.4:9223"]
      }
    }
  }
}
```
The OpenClaw gateway strips unknown top-level keys during MinIO sync. `mcp-keeper.sh` re-injects this config when it disappears. `fix-element-config.sh` installs an `mc` wrapper that injects it on every `mc cp` touching `openclaw.json`.

## 9. Key Constraints and Design Decisions

### Workspace is a shared mutable file system

The bind mount makes `/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/` visible to both containers and the host simultaneously. Three concurrent writers (`manager-config-keeper.sh`, the controller reconciler, and the OpenClaw gateway) write `openclaw.json` without coordination. The keeper's atomic write + `config-health.json` update is the only synchronization mechanism. This is a known constraint, not a bug to fix.

### hiclaw-manager and hiclaw-controller are not in Coolify

The containers require a shared volume mount (`/worksp/hiclaw/workspace`) that Coolify's compose model does not accommodate for this image version, plus startup-time environment injection and config patching baked into the keeper scripts. Migrating to Coolify would require rewriting the startup logic as compose-compatible entrypoints. This is deferred work.

### openclaw.json reconciler conflicts are structural

The hiclaw-controller ManagerReconciler writes its own template values to `openclaw.json` every ~47 seconds. This behavior is inside the upstream image and cannot be changed. The keeper scripts are the permanent mitigation: they run after the reconciler, normalize the schema, and update `config-health.json` to prevent observe-recovery from reverting the fixes.

### MinIO recursion is a standing hazard

The controller's reconciler pushes the workspace to MinIO. Because the manager's startup pull writes into the same workspace, if the exclusions are removed or circumvented, the workspace will contain a MinIO path that gets pushed back, creating recursive nesting. The `--exclude "hiclaw/*"` guard in `start-manager-agent.sh` is the only thing preventing this. Do not remove it.

### cdp_proxy.py must be edited in-place

`cdp_proxy.py` is bind-mounted into `novnc-desktop`. Docker bind mounts track the inode, not the path. Replacing the file (using Write tool, `cp`, or `mv`) creates a new inode; the container continues reading from the original inode and never sees the update. Always edit using the Edit tool (in-place modification) or `sed -i`.

### commands.restart baseline must match reconciler output

The gateway records its startup config as the baseline in `config-health.json` and triggers SIGUSR1 on any diff against that baseline. If the startup config has `commands.restart=true` and the keeper later writes `commands:{}`, the next reconciler write of `commands.restart=true` appears as no change (matches startup baseline) — correct. If the startup config has `commands:{}` (keeper ran first), a reconciler write of `commands.restart=true` appears as a diff — wrong, triggers restart loop. The startup script always sets `commands.restart=true` before starting the gateway; the keeper normalizes to `{}` afterward.

### YOLO settings removed from startup config

Earlier versions of `start-manager-agent.sh` wrote `tools.exec`, `tools.elevated`, and `agents.defaults.elevatedDefault` to `openclaw.json` at startup. The v1.1.2 ManagerReconciler writes `null` for these fields approximately every 47 seconds. This produced a continuous diff → SIGUSR1 → restart loop making control.claw unavailable ~40% of the time. These settings were removed from the startup write; the reconciler baseline now matches the reconciler output.

### Auto-login uses localStorage injection, not loginToken

The `loginToken` URL parameter creates a new Matrix device on every use, triggering Element's cross-signing verification screen. There is no programmatic skip path. The fixed `device_id=hiclaw_web_auto` approach reuses the same device record, bypassing verification entirely after the first login. This is specific to single-admin deployments.

## 10. Scope Boundary

**Owned by this repo:**
- `novnc-desktop/` — Docker image (Chrome, noVNC, CDP proxy)
- All `.sh` scripts at repo root — keeper and startup scripts
- `oauth2-proxy/` — auth proxy config
- `traefik/claw.yml` — routing rules

**Not owned (upstream, read-only):**

- OpenClaw gateway inside hiclaw-manager
- Tuwunel internal state at `/data/tuwunel/`
- MinIO data model and reconciliation logic

**Ephemeral (in-container, lost on restart, recreated by startup scripts):**
- clawtalk `index.cjs` wrapper
- Fake `/usr/local/bin/systemd-run`
- `/usr/local/bin/openclaw` symlink
- ClawTalk bundled shim at `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/clawtalk/`
- Element Web `config.json`, all injected JS files, nginx configs (regenerated by `start-element-web.sh`)

Model IDs here are Higress gateway alias IDs, not OpenRouter IDs. The startup script renames canonical OpenRouter IDs (e.g. `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`) to the alias format for all other models, but `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` is the one model whose ID matches an OpenRouter catalog entry and therefore receives live context window metadata. All other models keep static values from `known-models.json`.

The OpenClaw schema rejects unknown model fields. The startup script strips the `pricing` field from every model (returned by the OpenRouter sync) before writing to `openclaw.json`. Do not add `pricing` to model entries.

### `agents`

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "bootstrapMaxChars": 20000,
    "maxConcurrent": 4,
    "model": { "primary": "hiclaw-gateway/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro" },
    "models": {
      "hiclaw-gateway/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro": { "alias": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro" },
      ... (one entry per model above)
    },
    "memorySearch": {
      "model": "text-embedding-v4",
      "provider": "openai",
      "remote": {
        "apiKey": "[REDACTED]",
        "baseUrl": "http://aigw-local.hiclaw.io:8080/v1"
      }
    },
    "subagents": { "maxConcurrent": 8 },
    "timeoutSeconds": 1800,
    "workspace": "~"
  }
}
```

`bootstrapMaxChars: 20000` is enforced by the config keeper. The default OpenClaw value (8000) truncates AGENTS.md (15 KB+). Do not reduce this.

`agents.defaults.models` is rebuilt by the startup script from the full model list. Each entry maps the qualified ID (`hiclaw-gateway/<model-id>`) to a short `alias` used in the UI dropdown.

`memorySearch` is only present when `HICLAW_EMBEDDING_MODEL` is set. The remote block uses the same gateway key and AI gateway URL as `models.providers.hiclaw-gateway`.

### `plugins`

```json
"plugins": {
  "allow": ["whatsapp", "clawtalk"],
  "entries": {
    "matrix": { "enabled": true },
    "memory-core": {
      "enabled": true,
      "config": { "dreaming": { "enabled": true } }
    },
    "clawtalk": {
      "enabled": true,
      "config": {
        "apiKey": "[REDACTED]",
        "autoConnect": true
      }
    }
  },
  "load": {
    "paths": ["/opt/openclaw/extensions/matrix"]
  }
}
```

`plugins.load.paths` must not contain the clawtalk npm path (`/root/manager-workspace/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/clawtalk`). The config keeper removes it if present. ClawTalk loads from the bundled shim created by the startup script at `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/clawtalk/`.

The clawtalk `apiKey` (`cc_live_d5a5025bc0dc6894ac8acc6f867b336667e3e104`) is hardcoded in `manager-config-keeper.sh`. It is a service account key for the ClawTalk relay, not a user credential.

If WhatsApp is bootstrapped, additional entries appear:
```json
"plugins": {
  "allow": ["whatsapp", "clawtalk"],
  "load": { "paths": ["/root/manager-workspace/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/whatsapp"] }
},
"channels": {
  "whatsapp": { "enabled": true, "dmPolicy": "pairing", "groupPolicy": "allowlist" }
}
```

### `mcp`

```json
"mcp": {
  "servers": {
    "browser": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp", "--cdp-endpoint", "http://10.0.5.4:9223"]
    }
  }
}
```

The CDP endpoint `10.0.5.4:9223` is the static IP of `novnc-desktop` on the `e10kwzww46ljhrgz1qj08j6a` Docker network, proxied through `cdp_proxy.py` from Chrome's internal port 9222. This block enables the Playwright MCP browser tool inside the manager agent.

The gateway may strip the `mcp` key during a MinIO sync cycle. `mcp-keeper.sh` re-adds it when absent. `mcp-keeper.sh` is not in cron — run it manually: `bash /worksp/hiclaw/mcp-keeper.sh`.

## Keeper-Managed Fields

`manager-config-keeper.sh` runs every minute from host cron and enforces these invariants:

| Field | Enforced value | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `commands` | `{"restart": true}` | Matches startup baseline; prevents gateway restart loop on every reconciler write |
| `session.dmScope` | `"main"` | Keeps HiClaw chat and OpenClaw web chat on one shared session |
| `channels.matrix.groups["*"]` | Removed if present | OpenClaw schema rejects wildcard key; its presence breaks all config reloads |
| `channels.matrix.groups[*].allow` | Renamed to `enabled` | Controller writes `allow`; schema requires `enabled` |
| `plugins.load.paths` | Clawtalk npm path removed | ClawTalk loads from bundled shim, not npm path |
| `plugins.entries.clawtalk` | Added if absent | Ensures ClawTalk is always registered |
| `agents.defaults.bootstrapMaxChars` | `20000` | Prevents AGENTS.md truncation at the default 8000-char limit |
| `models.providers.hiclaw-gateway[deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro].contextWindow` | `1048576` | Reconciler restores stale values from its internal state |
| `models.providers.hiclaw-gateway[deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash].contextWindow` | `1048576` | Same |

When the keeper makes changes, it also:
1. Deletes `workspace/openclaw.json.bak` — prevents observe-recovery from reverting the fix
2. Updates `config-health.json` with a fresh hash, byte count, and file metadata — prevents observe-recovery from flagging the keeper's version as "unexpected" and reverting it

---

## Startup Script Dynamic Fields

`start-manager-agent.sh` overwrites these fields on every container start:

| Field | Source |
|---|---|
| `channels.matrix.accessToken` | Fresh Matrix login token from `POST /login` to Tuwunel |
| `channels.matrix.homeserver` | `HICLAW_MATRIX_URL` env var |
| `channels.matrix.userId` | Constructed from `HICLAW_MATRIX_DOMAIN` |
| `models.providers.hiclaw-gateway.apiKey` | `HICLAW_MANAGER_GATEWAY_KEY` |
| `models.providers.hiclaw-gateway.baseUrl` | `HICLAW_AI_GATEWAY_URL` |
| `models[*].contextWindow` and `models[*].maxTokens` | OpenRouter API (for matching models only) |
| `gateway.auth.token`, `gateway.remote.token` | `HICLAW_MANAGER_GATEWAY_KEY` |
| `agents.defaults.model.primary` | `HICLAW_DEFAULT_MODEL` |
| `agents.defaults.models` | Rebuilt from full model list |
| `agents.defaults.memorySearch` | `HICLAW_EMBEDDING_MODEL` and `HICLAW_AI_GATEWAY_URL` |
| `commands` | Cleared (`del(.commands.restart)`) — keeper then writes `{restart:true}` |

After the OpenRouter sync, the startup script immediately pushes the updated `openclaw.json` to MinIO so the background MinIO-to-Local sync loop (which starts a few seconds later) does not overwrite the fresh values with stale MinIO data.

## openclaw Install Paths

| Path | When present |
|---|---|
| `/opt/openclaw/` | Always — bundled in the base image |
| `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/` | After a successful `openclaw update` via the keeper |

The startup script validates the npm install before symlinking it. Both `node_modules/json5/package.json` and `node_modules/openai/index.mjs` must exist. A partial OOM-interrupted npm install leaves `openclaw.mjs` present but all `.mjs` files absent from the `openai` package — `node openclaw.mjs --version` succeeds but the gateway crashes on first use. If either check fails, the broken install is removed and the symlink falls back to the base image version.

The package hash is written to `workspace/.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash` on every start. `manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh` polls this file and restarts the container when the hash changes (indicating a successful `openclaw update` completed).

---

## openclaw Update Mechanism

Triggered by clicking "Update now" in the Control UI at `control.claw.designflow.app`.

1. openclaw's `update.run` invokes `systemd-run --user --scope` to spawn a detached update helper.
2. The startup script installs a fake `/usr/local/bin/systemd-run` that writes `/root/manager-workspace/.openclaw-update-requested` (visible on the host at `workspace/.openclaw-update-requested`) and exits 0.
3. openclaw believes the handoff started successfully and sends itself SIGUSR1 (in-process restart). This is expected — it kills the active agent session.
4. `manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh` (cron, every minute) detects the marker file, removes it, then runs: `docker exec hiclaw-manager openclaw update --yes --json`
5. The keeper sleeps 30 seconds after the update completes to allow openclaw's in-process SIGUSR1 restart (which rewrites `openclaw.json`) to finish before `docker restart` is called. **This sleep is critical** — without it, `docker restart` kills the container mid-write and produces a truncated `openclaw.json` (the 8788-byte truncation incident, 2026-05-31).
6. The keeper detects the changed package hash and calls `docker restart hiclaw-manager` to load the new binary.

Do not run `openclaw update` directly inside the container. The keeper handles memory limits (`--memory-swap 3g` via `docker update`) that are required to prevent npm OOM mid-install.

## MinIO Storage Configuration

MinIO runs inside `hiclaw-controller` on port 9000.

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Endpoint (from manager) | `http://hiclaw-controller:9000` |
| Endpoint (from controller) | `http://127.0.0.1:9000` |
| Access key | `default` |
| Secret key | `HICLAW_FS_SECRET_KEY` |
| Bucket | `hiclaw-storage` |
| mc alias | `hiclaw` |

In mc path syntax: `hiclaw/hiclaw-storage/manager/openclaw.json` = alias `hiclaw`, bucket `hiclaw-storage`, object path `manager/openclaw.json`.

### Bucket Layout

| Prefix | Contents |
|---|---|
| `manager/` | Manager workspace: `openclaw.json`, agent state, extensions, credentials |
| `agents/` | Shared agent state across manager + workers |
| `shared/` | Cross-instance shared files |
| `hiclaw-config/` | k8s-mode config files pulled by background sync every 15 seconds |

### MinIO Sync Exclusions (Critical)

The k8s startup block in `start-manager-agent.sh` uses `mc mirror` with these `--exclude` flags. Do not remove any of them:

| Exclusion | Reason |
|---|---|
| `hiclaw/*` | Prevents recursive storage explosion: the controller pushes the local MinIO mirror wholesale; pulling it into the workspace creates `manager/hiclaw/hiclaw-storage/manager/hiclaw/...` infinite nesting (Incident 2026-05-20) |
| `hiclaw-fs` | Container-internal symlink to `/root/hiclaw-fs`; recreated fresh on each start |
| `*.clobbered.*` | Observe-recovery forensic backup files — runtime noise |
| `.npm/*` | npm cache — large, runtime-only |
| `.codex/*` | Codex session state — runtime-only |
| `.cache/*` | Generic cache directories |

## Traefik Dynamic Config

File: `traefik/claw.yml` (copy of the live `/data/coolify/proxy/dynamic/claw.yml`)

To apply changes: `docker cp traefik/claw.yml coolify-proxy:/traefik/dynamic/claw.yml`
Traefik hot-reloads this file automatically; no container restart is needed.

### Middlewares

| Name | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `google-auth` | `forwardAuth` | Forwards requests to `http://oauth2-proxy:4180` for Google OAuth validation. Passes `X-Auth-Request-User` and `X-Auth-Request-Email` headers on success. |

### Routers and Services

| Domain / Path | Router | Service | Auth | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `claw.designflow.app` (excl. `/_matrix`, `/_synapse`) | `hiclaw-element` | `http://10.0.1.1:18088` | `google-auth` | Element Web (hosted on controller port 18088, reached via host IP) |
| `claw.designflow.app/_matrix` or `/_synapse` | `hiclaw-matrix` | `http://10.0.1.1:18080` | None | Matrix federation + client API (priority 20, no auth) |
| `control.claw.designflow.app` | `hiclaw-control` | `http://hiclaw-controller:18888` | `google-auth` | Control UI — routed to controller nginx (port 18888), which proxies to manager port 18799 |
| `control.claw.designflow.app/oauth2` | `hiclaw-oauth2` | `http://oauth2-proxy:4180` | None | OAuth2 callback endpoint (priority 30, no auth) |
| `gateway.claw.designflow.app` | `hiclaw-gateway` | `http://hiclaw-manager:18799` | `google-auth` | Direct gateway access (certResolver: letsencrypt-dns) |
| `vnc.designflow.app` | `hiclaw-vnc` | `http://novnc-desktop:3000` | None | noVNC desktop access (no auth) |

Note: `hiclaw-control-svc` routes to `hiclaw-controller:18888`. The controller does not bind port 18888 externally — it listens internally on 18888 via the nginx `manager-console.conf`. Traefik reaches it over the `hiclaw-net` Docker network.

## nginx Configs (inside hiclaw-controller)

Generated by `start-element-web.sh` at every container start. Not static files — changes require editing `start-element-web.sh` and restarting the controller.

### `manager-console.conf` (port 18888)

Proxies `control.claw.designflow.app` to the manager's OpenClaw gateway at `hiclaw-manager:18799`. Key behaviors:

- **Docker DNS resolver** (`127.0.0.11 valid=10s`): The upstream hostname is stored in a `$upstream` variable rather than used directly. This forces nginx to re-resolve `hiclaw-manager` on each request, so IP changes after `docker restart hiclaw-manager` are transparent. Do not replace with a hardcoded IP — doing so causes 502 errors after every manager container recreation.
- **Auto-login token injection**: A `sub_filter` injects an inline `<script>` that sets `location.hash` to `#token=[REDACTED], not from a cookie or stored credential.
- **CSP stripping**: `proxy_hide_header Content-Security-Policy` allows the injected inline script to execute.
- **WebSocket support**: `proxy_set_header Upgrade`, `Connection "upgrade"`, and extended timeouts (3600s) support the gateway's real-time WebSocket connections.

### `element-web.conf` (port 8088)

Serves the Element Web SPA. Key behaviors:

- **JS injection**: `sub_filter '</head>'` appends five scripts: `browser-bypass.js` (sets `mx_accepts_unsupported_browser`), `auto-login.js` (injects Matrix session into localStorage), `auth-ui-tweaks.js` (styles SSO button, auto-clicks skip-verification), `control-panel-btn.js` (fixed "Control Panel" button), `new-chat-btn.js` ("+ New Chat" button).
- **hiclaw-chat-api.py proxy**: Locations `/hiclaw-api/new-chat`, `/hiclaw-api/session`, `/hiclaw-api/matrix-auth`, `/hiclaw-api/healthz` proxy to the Python API server at `127.0.0.1:8091`.
- **config.json**: Served with `no-cache`; a `sub_filter` replaces `__PUBLIC_BASE_URL__` with the current request scheme and host, so the homeserver URL adapts correctly when accessed from different domains.

### `plugin-server.conf` (port 8002)

Serves Higress WASM plugin binaries from `/usr/share/nginx/html/plugins/` to Envoy's filter-chain loader. Required for AI plugins (LLM request rewriting) to function. If this server is down, Envoy cannot fetch plugin bytecode and all AI gateway requests fail.

---

## `config-health.json`

Path: `workspace/.openclaw/logs/config-health.json`

This file is OpenClaw's config-health baseline. It records the hash, byte count, and filesystem metadata of `openclaw.json` at the time the gateway last processed it as "last known good". The observe-recovery mechanism uses this baseline to detect and revert "unexpected" changes.

`manager-config-keeper.sh` updates this file atomically whenever it writes `openclaw.json`. If `config-health.json` is not updated to match a keeper-written `openclaw.json`, observe-recovery will flag the keeper's version as unexpected and revert it within minutes.

`start-manager-agent.sh` deletes `config-health.json` entirely at startup so the gateway establishes a fresh baseline from the startup-patched `openclaw.json`. This ensures the startup script's changes (Matrix token, model metadata, `commands.restart`) become the new baseline rather than being reverted.

---

## Cron Configuration

The always-on keeper scripts run every minute from the `ai` user's crontab:

```cron
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/manager-config-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/manager-config-keeper.log 2>&1
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/manager-bootstrap-keeper.log 2>&1
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/controller-bootstrap-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/controller-bootstrap-keeper.log 2>&1
* * * * * /worksp/hiclaw/novnc-resource-keeper.sh >> /worksp/hiclaw/novnc-resource-keeper.log 2>&1
```

Verify the crontab is active: `sudo crontab -u ai -l`

`mcp-keeper.sh` is not in cron. Run it manually when the browser MCP tool disappears from the agent:

bash /worksp/hiclaw/mcp-keeper.sh
```

## Known Configuration Smells

- **ClawTalk API key is [REDACTED] in `manager-config-keeper.sh` (`cc_live_d5a5025bc0dc6894ac8acc6f867b336667e3e104`). It belongs in an env var or secret store. Accepted for a single-deployment host-ops layer.
- **`fix-element-config.sh` hardcodes the gateway key** in the nginx `manager-console.conf` sub_filter for auto-login. The key is already in the manager container's environment — the hardcoded value is a convenience for the one-off repair script only.
- **OpenRouter model sync is partial.** Only `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` has a matching OpenRouter model ID and receives live context window values. All Higress gateway alias IDs (`gpt-5.4`, `claude-opus-4-6`, etc.) have no OpenRouter equivalent and keep static values from `known-models.json`.
- **`gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth = true`** and **`allowInsecureAuth = true`** are required for the auto-login token injection to work. These flags are acceptable on a server gated by Google OAuth at the Traefik layer, but they should not be present in a multi-user deployment.
- **`oauth2-proxy/.env` contains real credentials** and is committed. This is the current single-server deployment approach. Rotate credentials by updating this file and restarting the container.

### Incident 3 — Chrome double-instance OOM crash (2026-05-08)

**Root cause 1:** Chrome watchdog used `pkill -f "chrome\|pattern"` — `\|` in ERE is literal pipe, not alternation. pkill matched nothing; old Chrome survived.

**Root cause 2:** Chrome wrapper unconditionally deleted Singleton files before launch. Dropbox OAuth callback opened `google-chrome https://...` → second full Chrome instance → 2.2 GB combined RSS → OOM.

**Fix:** Changed `\|` to `|` in watchdog pkill. Added pgrep guard: only delete Singleton files when Chrome is not running.

---

### Incident 4 — MinIO recursive storage path / server crash (2026-05-20)

**Root cause:** `HICLAW_RUNTIME=k8s` causes the k8s startup block to run `mc mirror hiclaw/hiclaw-storage/manager/ /root/manager-workspace/ --overwrite`. The workspace is bind-mounted into hiclaw-controller as `/root/hiclaw-fs/agents/manager/`. The ManagerReconciler pushes the workspace back to MinIO as `hiclaw/hiclaw-storage/manager/`. Since the mirror pull brought `hiclaw/hiclaw-storage/` INTO the workspace, the push created a nested copy. Each restart cycle added one more nesting level until disk usage exploded.

**Fix:** Added `--exclude` guards to the k8s `mc mirror` call: `hiclaw/*`, `hiclaw-fs`, `*.clobbered.*`, `.npm/*`, `.codex/*`, `.cache/*`.

**Cleanup check:**
```bash
sudo find /var/lib/docker/volumes/hiclaw-data/_data/minio/hiclaw-storage \
  -maxdepth 8 -type d -name "hiclaw-storage" -print
# Should print only the root path. Any extra lines = recursion returning.
```

---

### Incident 5 — clawtalk plugin lost on container restart (resolved 2026-05-08)

**Root cause:** CJS wrapper for the clawtalk ESM plugin lived in the container overlay. Lost on restart.

**Fix:** `bootstrap_clawtalk_plugin()` in `start-manager-agent.sh` recreates the shim and clears `installs.json` on every container start. `bot_connected ✓` verified.

---

### Incident 6 — Swap exhausted by stale Claude Code session; npm OOM crash loop (2026-05-30)

**Root cause:** A 9-day-old abandoned bash session (PID 527449) was consuming 828 MB swap. Combined with npm install peak memory, OOM killed mid-install. Partial install left json5 missing → persistent gateway crash loop.

**Fix:** Killed stale process (freed 828 MB swap). Recreated container from base image. Updated via UI update path.

**Rule:** Do NOT run `npm install -g openclaw@latest` directly. Use "Update now" UI button.

---

### Incident 7 — openclaw hash detection broken: container overlay vs. workspace volume (2026-05-30)

**Root cause:** Startup script wrote hash to `${HOME}/.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash` (inside container overlay, invisible to host). Keeper read from `/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash` (always empty). Hash comparison always skipped; version changes never triggered docker restart.

**Fix:** Changed startup script to write hash to `/root/manager-workspace/.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash` (bind-mounted path, visible to host at `/worksp/hiclaw/workspace/.openclaw-startup-pkg-hash`).

**Rule:** Any state the keeper needs must be written to a bind-mounted path. Never write keeper-readable state to `/root/` or `${HOME}/` container paths.

---

### Incident 8 — control.claw 502 Bad Gateway after manager container recreation (2026-05-30)

**Root cause:** `manager-console.conf` used hardcoded container IP. `docker rm + run` gave the new container a different IP from Docker's DHCP pool.

**Fix:** `start-element-web.sh` generates the nginx config with `resolver 127.0.0.11 valid=10s; set $upstream hiclaw-manager; proxy_pass http://$upstream:18799;` — Docker DNS re-resolves on each request.

---

### Incident 9 — OpenRouter sync poisoned config with 'pricing' field (2026-05-30)

**Root cause:** Early sync expression spread the full OpenRouter model object into openclaw.json. The `pricing` field failed OpenClaw's strict schema validation, crashing the gateway on every start and losing all config customizations.

**Fix:** Sync expression extracts only `contextWindow` and `maxTokens`. Defensive `del(.pricing)` pass runs on every startup.

---

### Incident 10 — openclaw update.run always returning managed-service-handoff-unavailable (2026-05-31)

**Root causes:**
1. `channels.matrix.groups["*"]` wildcard key failed schema validation → every `update.run` rejected
2. `manager-config-keeper.sh` deleting wrong `.bak` path → observe-recovery kept restoring stale config with `"*"` key
3. No systemd in Docker → `detectRespawnSupervisor()` returned null → handoff path unavailable

**Fix:** Keeper strips `"*"` key. Correct `.bak` path. Fake systemd-run + `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` + keeper-based marker consumption.

---

### Incident 11 — openclaw downgraded after container recreation; npm OOM crash loop (2026-05-31)

**Root causes:**
1. `docker rm` destroyed npm overlay → fell back to base image v2026.4.14
2. `--memory-swap 768m` = 0 swap available → npm install OOM-killed mid-install
3. Startup validation only checked json5 (not openai) → broken install accepted → gateway crashed

**Fix:** `--memory-swap 3g` (via `--memory 1536m`). Dual validation: json5 + openai/index.mjs. Fallback to base image on failed validation.

---

### Incident 12 — openclaw.json truncation loop: 103+ files at 8788 bytes (2026-05-31)

**Root cause:** `manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh` ran `openclaw update --yes --json`, then immediately ran `docker restart hiclaw-manager`. openclaw's in-process SIGUSR1 restart was still mid-write to `openclaw.json` when the container was killed. The write was consistently interrupted at 8788 bytes (vs full 9283 bytes). This happened 103 times over ~7.5 hours, producing 211 `.clobbered.*` forensic files.

**Evidence:** `workspace/openclaw.json.clobbered.*` — 103 files at exactly 8788 bytes, timestamps from 2026-05-31T16:50 to 2026-06-01T00:19.

**Fix (committed a93488c):** `sleep 30` inserted in `manager-bootstrap-keeper.sh` between `openclaw update` completion and the hash-check + docker restart block.

**Rule:** Never docker restart hiclaw-manager immediately after openclaw update. The 30-second window is required for the in-process SIGUSR1 write to complete.

---

### Incident 13 — openai/index.mjs missing from partial npm install (2026-06-01)

**Root cause:** Different OOM failure mode from Incident 11: the `openai` package ended up with all `.mjs` files absent but `.map` files intact. `json5/package.json` was present, so the validation passed. Gateway crashed on first request with `Cannot find module openai/index.mjs`. Control.claw returned 502.

**Fix (committed 57ff7c1):** Added second validation check: `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/openai/index.mjs` must exist. If absent: remove broken install, fall back to `/opt/openclaw/`.

---

### Incident 14 — 502 on control.claw from jq parse errors + YOLO reconciler diff loop (2026-05-31)

**Root causes:**
1. `start-manager-agent.sh` had inline bash comments (`# comment`) inside jq string expressions. jq parsed these as jq comments but broke the surrounding pipeline context → `jq: parse error: Unfinished JSON term at EOF at line 344` on every keeper run → startup script never completed config patching → config drift never fixed
2. The YOLO settings block (`tools.exec`, `tools.elevated`, `agents.defaults.elevatedDefault`) was written to openclaw.json at startup. The v1.1.2 ManagerReconciler writes null for these fields every ~47 seconds → constant diff → SIGUSR1 → restart cycle → control.claw unavailable ~40% of the time
3. openclaw 2026.5.28 was OOM-crashing with fatal heap limit at 768m RAM every ~45 seconds

**Fix (commits daedd9e, b5cf73b, c3a3319):** Removed inline comments from jq expressions. Removed the YOLO defaults block entirely so the reconciler baseline matches startup config. Increased manager container RAM from 768m to 1536m, swap from 2g to 3g.

---

### Incident 15 — host-wide OOM from uncapped novnc-desktop Chrome/QtWebEngine (2026-06-03)

**Root cause:** `novnc-desktop` ran Chrome/QtWebEngine without Docker memory or PID limits. During the crash, kernel OOM logs showed global OOM (`constraint=CONSTRAINT_NONE`) killing `chrome` and `QtWebEngineProc` in the noVNC cgroup. The container had 444 PIDs in a later snapshot and the host swapfile was only 4 GiB, which saturated and made SSH appear unreachable.

**Fix:** `novnc-desktop/recreate.sh` now starts the container with `--memory 3g --memory-swap 4g --cpus 2 --pids-limit 250`. `novnc-resource-keeper.sh` runs every minute from the `ai` crontab, re-applies those limits after recreation, and restarts the container before memory or PID count reaches the danger zone. Host swap was resized to 12 GiB and `vm.swappiness=20` was persisted in `/etc/sysctl.d/99-hiclaw-memory.conf`.

**Rule:** Never run `novnc-desktop` uncapped. If the desktop is intentionally unused, leave it stopped; the keeper will not start it. If it is started for browser automation, the Docker limits and keeper must be active first.

---

## 16. Pending Work

- [x] Clawtalk loads automatically on container start
- [x] openclaw update detection fixed (hash written to workspace volume)
- [x] control.claw 502 after manager restart fixed (Docker DNS in nginx)
- [x] openclaw symlink fixed after npm update (dual validation: json5 + openai/index.mjs)
- [x] OpenRouter model metadata sync (deepseek contextWindow 1048576)
- [x] openclaw update.run fixed (fake systemd-run + OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT + marker flow)
- [x] npm OOM during openclaw update fixed (--memory-swap 3g)
- [x] openclaw.json truncation race fixed (sleep 30 in keeper)
- [x] jq parse errors from inline comments fixed
- [x] YOLO reconciler diff loop fixed (removed YOLO block from startup)
- [x] Manager RAM increased to 1536m (no more heap-limit OOM at 768m)
- [x] novnc-desktop host-wide OOM guard added (3g RAM, 4g total swap, 250 PIDs, keeper cron)
- [ ] Other models still use hardcoded context windows — gpt-5.4, claude-opus-4-6, kimi-k2.5, etc. use Higress gateway alias IDs with no OpenRouter equivalent. Need a mapping table or canonical ID migration.
- [ ] Rebuild `ghcr.io/u2giants/novnc-desktop` image — Chrome wrapper fix (pgrep guard) is in the source; new image has not been built/pushed since the fix.
- [ ] Mount clawtalk modifications from host — currently recreated inside the container by the bootstrap. Should be a host-mounted bind so changes survive container recreation without a script update.
- [ ] Move hiclaw-manager and hiclaw-controller to Coolify — low priority; keepers work reliably.
- [ ] Move oauth2-proxy to Coolify — low priority.
- [ ] Verify tuwunel (Matrix homeserver) status — `start-tuwunel.sh` exists; confirm it is running and healthy.
- [ ] Set up git pull automation on server — script changes require manual `git pull` after push.
- [ ] Clean up 211 `.clobbered.*` files from workspace if disk space is needed.

payload.message: `AGENTS.md` is currently very detailed but too long and not in the requested 14-section shape. I’m going to replace it with a shorter canonical operating guide, keeping the hard-won quirks and incident rules but moving deep procedural detail to the focused docs.
